mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. This mechanism operates continuously whenever rate changes occur.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.08% suggests potential easing cycle ahead
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation between FEDFUNDS monthly changes and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which sustains corporate revenue expansion and equity valuations. The mechanism triggers when monthly payroll growth exceeds 150,000 jobs.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 153,308 thousand suggests near-trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects consumer spending strength and forward economic momentum. When retail sales accelerate above trend, equity markets price in sustained corporate earnings growth over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day return differential between high and low RSAFS growth regimes is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion. Rising INDPRO indicates improving operational efficiency and profit margins, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth (Spearman rho p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. When unemployment rises above 4.0%, equity markets reprice downward due to expected earnings headwinds from weakened demand.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and mortgage demand (HOUST) interact with monetary conditions (FEDFUNDS) to determine real estate sector health and consumer wealth effects. When housing demand falls while rates remain elevated, equity valuations compress due to reduced collateral values and construction employment.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,439 thousand units, FEDFUNDS at 3.08%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing demand shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) jointly determine real interest rates and equity risk premiums. When inflation accelerates while the Fed maintains elevated rates, real rates rise and equity valuations contract over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or inflation persistence with policy constraint
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression predicting positive 5-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with retail sales momentum (RSAFS) indicates broad-based economic expansion. When both series show acceleration, equity markets sustain positive returns over the next month as earnings growth expectations rise across sectors.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20,021.721 billion dollars, RSAFS at 683,974 million dollars
regime_hint: broad-based economic expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or joint interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in predictive model.
